title: Get Started
text: echoplot contains various functions can used separately comprehensive echolocatoR::finemap_loci() pipeline. Generate multi-view plot given locus using echoplot::plot_locus(). can mix match different tracks annotations using different arguments (see ?plot_locus details). plot centred lead/index SNP. list supplied zoom, zoom view saved individually. modifications applied tracks . increase resolution adjusting dpi argument (default=300). Files saved jpg format default, users can specify preferred file format (e.g. file_format="png"). Adjust height width saved plot using respective arguments. plot automatically saved locus-specific directory : *multiview_<locus>_<zoom>.jpg*.

title: Get Started
text: Load example dataset results fine-mapping BST1 locus finemap_loci(). Original data comes recent Nalls et al. (2019) Parkinson’s disease GWAS (see ?BST1 details).
code: library(ggplot2) library(patchwork)  root.dir <- tempdir() locus_dir <- file.path(root.dir, echodata::locus_dir) dat <- echodata::BST1 LD_matrix <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix LD_reference <- "UKB" # Used for naming saved plots zoom <- "10x" show_plot <- FALSE

title: Get Started
code: trk_plot <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                  LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                  LD_reference = LD_reference,                                  locus_dir = locus_dir,                                  save_plot = FALSE,                                  show_plot = show_plot,                                  zoom = zoom) methods::show(trk_plot)

title: Get Started
text: can easily generate locus plot multiple zoomed views supplying list zoom. list can composed zoom multipliers (e.g. c("1x", "2x")), window widths units basepairs (e.g. c(5000, 1500)), mixture (e.g. c("1x","4x", 5000, 2000)). zoom view saved individually respective scale suffix (e.g. multiview.BST1.UKB.4x.jpg). zoom view stored named item within returned list.
code: trk_zooms <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                   LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                   LD_reference = LD_reference,                                   locus_dir = locus_dir,                                   save_plot = FALSE,                                   show_plot = show_plot,                                   zoom = c("1x", "5x", "10x")) names(trk_zooms) # Get zoom view names methods::show(trk_zooms)

title: Get Started
text: even control track multi-view plot, specify plot_locus(..., return_list=TRUE) instead return named list (nested within zoom view list item) ggplot objects can modified individually. ’ve made modifications, can bind list plots back together patchwork::wrap_plots(tracks_list, ncol = 1). Modify specific track within view.
code: trk_plot_list <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                       LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                       LD_reference = LD_reference,                                       locus_dir = locus_dir,                                       save_plot = FALSE,                                       show_plot = show_plot,                                       zoom = zoom,                                       return_list = TRUE) view1_list <- trk_plot_list[[zoom]] names(view1_list) # Get track names from a particular zoom view # Modify your selected track modified_track <- view1_list$GWAS +                       ggplot2::labs(title = "Modified GWAS") +                       ggplot2::theme_dark() +                       ggplot2::theme(title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = .5)) # Put it back into your track list view1_list[["GWAS"]] <- modified_track # Remove a plot you don't want view1_list[["Genes"]] <- NULL # Specify the relative heights of each track track_heights <- c(.3, .1, .3, 1)  # Bind them together and plot fused_plot <- patchwork::wrap_plots(view1_list,                                     heights = track_heights,                                     ncol = 1) methods::show(fused_plot)

title: Get Started
text: Whenever use annotation arguments (e.g. xgr_libnames, Roadmap, nott_epigenome) annotations overlap locus automatically saved GRanges objects locus-specific subdirectory: results/<dataset_type>/<dataset_name>/<locus>/annotation selected annotation previously downloaded stored locus, plot_locus() automatically detect import save time.
code: trk_plot.xgr <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                      LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                      LD_reference = LD_reference,                                      locus_dir = locus_dir,                                      xgr_libnames = c("ENCODE_TFBS_ClusteredV3_CellTypes"),                                      save_plot = FALSE,                                      show_plot = show_plot,                                      zoom = zoom) methods::show(trk_plot.xgr)

title: Get Started
text: Using Roadmap=TRUE roadmap_query="<query>" arguments searches Roadmap chromatin mark data across various cell-types, cell-lines tissues. Note Roadmap queries require tabix installed machine, within conda environment (conda_env = "echoR"). Parallelising queries across multiple threads speeds process (nThread=<n_cores_available>), reusing previously stored data automatically saved locus-specific subfolder. NOTE: Querying remote genomic data sources (e.g. Roadmap bigwigs) currently supported Windows due known bugs rtracklayer.
code: if (.Platform$OS.type != "windows") {     trk_plot.roadmap <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                              LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                              LD_reference = LD_reference,                                              locus_dir = locus_dir,                                              roadmap = TRUE,                                              roadmap_query = "monocyte",                                              save_plot = FALSE,                                              show_plot = show_plot,                                              zoom = "5x") } methods::show(trk_plot.roadmap)

title: Get Started
text: Query plot brain cell type-specific epigenomic assays Nott et al. (Science, 2019) (see ?NOTT_2019.bigwig_metadata details).
code: trk_plot.nott_2019 <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat,                                            LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                                            LD_reference = LD_reference,                                            locus_dir = locus_dir,                                            nott_epigenome = TRUE,                                            nott_binwidth = 200,                                            nott_regulatory_rects = TRUE,                                            nott_show_placseq = TRUE,                                            save_plot = FALSE,                                            show_plot = show_plot,                                            zoom = zoom) methods::show(trk_plot.nott_2019)

title: Get Started
text: Plot multiple QTL p-value columns (really P-value columns kind dataset). QTL dataset plotted new track.
code: dat1 <- data.table::copy(dat) dat2 <- data.table::copy(dat) # Make fake QTL P-values for the sake of demonstration dat1$P <- abs(jitter(dat1$P, amount = 1e-15)) dat2$P <- abs(jitter(dat2$P, amount = 1e-16)) dat_ls <- list("fake_eQTL" = dat1,                "fake_sQTL" = dat2)  trk_plot.qtl <- echoplot::plot_locus_multi(dat_ls = dat_ls,                                            LD_ls = list(LD_matrix, LD_matrix),                                            locus_dir = locus_dir,                                            show_plot = show_plot,                                            zoom = "10x") methods::show(trk_plot.qtl)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Brian Schilder. Author, maintainer. Jack Humphrey. Author. Towfique Raj. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Brian M Schilder, Jack Humphrey, Towfique Raj (2021). echolocatoR: automated end--end statistical functional genomic fine-mapping pipeline. Bioinformatics, btab658. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btab658
code: @Article{,   title = {echolocatoR: an automated end-to-end statistical and functional genomic fine-mapping pipeline},   author = {Brian M Schilder and Jack Humphrey and Towfique Raj},   journal = {Bioinformatics},   year = {2021},   volume = {btab658},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btab658}, }

what: echoplot: Echoverse module: Locus plot creation for fine-mapping and colocalization studies.
title: Echoverse Module: Locus Plot Creation for Fine-Mapping and Colocalization Studies
text: R package part echoverse suite supports echolocatoR: automated genomic fine-mapping pipeline. use echoplot, please cite: Brian M Schilder, Jack Humphrey, Towfique Raj (2021). echolocatoR: automated end--end statistical functional genomic fine-mapping pipeline. Bioinformatics, btab658. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btab658

title: Echoverse Module: Locus Plot Creation for Fine-Mapping and Colocalization Studies
code: if(!require("BiocManager")) install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install("RajLabMSSM/echoplot") library(echoplot)

title: Echoverse Module: Locus Plot Creation for Fine-Mapping and Colocalization Studies
text: Brian M. Schilder, Bioinformatician IIRaj LabDepartment Neuroscience, Icahn School Medicine Mount Sinai

title: Plot annotation peaks: ROADMAP — ROADMAP_plot
text: Import annotation ROADMAP, filter within range dat, plot peaks. annotation already downloaded previously, reused.

title: Plot annotation peaks: ROADMAP — ROADMAP_plot
code: ROADMAP_plot(   dat,   roadmap_query,   lib_name = "Roadmap.ChromatinMarks_CellTypes",   locus_dir = tempdir(),   limit_files = NULL,   n_top = 5,   adjust = 0.2,   force_new = FALSE,   show_plot = FALSE,   conda_env = "echoR_mini",   nThread = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot annotation peaks: ROADMAP — ROADMAP_plot
text: dat data.table least following columns: SNP SNP RSID CHR chromosome POS position roadmap_query Search columns Roadmap annotations metadata query annotations contain keywords. Can provide multiple keywords list form: c("placenta","liver","monocytes") lib_name Name data library use. locus_dir Locus-specific directory. limit_files Limit number annotation files queried (faster testing). n_top Number top annotations plotted (passed ROADMAP_query). adjust granularity peaks. force_new Download prepare new query even file already exists locally (Default: FALSE). show_plot Print plot. conda_env Conda environment search tabix . nThread Number threads parallelise downloading annotations . verbose Print messages.

title: Plot annotation peaks: ROADMAP — ROADMAP_plot
text: named list containing: "data" GRanges object within queried coordinates. "plot" ggbio plot.

title: Plot annotation peaks: ROADMAP — ROADMAP_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dat <- echodata::BST1[seq_len(1000),] roadmap_out <- echoplot::ROADMAP_plot(     dat = dat,     roadmap_query = "monocyte") } # }

title: Plot ROADMAP query — ROADMAP_track_plot
text: Plot ROADMAP query

title: Plot ROADMAP query — ROADMAP_track_plot
code: ROADMAP_track_plot(   grl.roadmap,   gr.snp = NULL,   geom = "density",   adjust = 0.2,   position = c("stack", "fill", "dodge"),   show_plot = TRUE,   as_ggplot = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot ROADMAP query — ROADMAP_track_plot
text: gr.snp <- echodata::dt_to_granges(echodata::BST1) grl.roadmap <- echoannot::ROADMAP_query(     query_dat = gr.snp,     merge_and_process = TRUE,     keyword_query = "monocyte") track.roadmap <- ROADMAP_track_plot(grl.roadmap,     gr.snp = gr.snp)

title: Plot ROADMAP query — ROADMAP_track_plot
text: grl.roadmap ROADMAP query results. gr.snp Optionally, can include extra GRanges object ensure plot extend beyond certain coordinates. geom type plot create. Options include "density" "histogram". adjust granularity peaks. show_plot Whether print plot.

title: Plot annotation peaks: XGR — XGR_plot
text: Import annotation XGR, filter within range dat, plot peaks. annotation already downloaded previously, reused.

title: Plot annotation peaks: XGR — XGR_plot
code: XGR_plot(   dat,   lib_name = "ENCODE_TFBS_ClusteredV3_CellTypes",   locus_dir = tempdir(),   palette = get_palettes(n_pals = 1, names_only = TRUE),   fill_var = "Assay",   facet_var = "Source",   geom = "density",   n_top = 5,   adjust = 0.2,   force_new = FALSE,   show_plot = FALSE,   nThread = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot annotation peaks: XGR — XGR_plot
text: dat data.table least following columns: SNP SNP RSID CHR chromosome POS position lib_name XGR annotations check overlap . full list libraries see   (XGR CRAN).  Passed RData.customised argument XGR::xRDataLoader. locus_dir Locus-specific directory. palette palette available pals. See get_palettes list palettes. fill_var Fill variable. facet_var Row facet variable. geom Plot type ("density", "histogram"). n_top Number top annotations plotted (passed XGR_filter_sources XGR_filter_assays). adjust granularity peaks. force_new Download prepare new query even file already exists locally (Default: FALSE). show_plot Print plot. nThread Number threads parallelise downloading annotations . verbose Print messages.

title: Plot annotation peaks: XGR — XGR_plot
text: List "data" "plot".

title: Plot annotation peaks: XGR — XGR_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ xgr_out <- echoplot::XGR_plot(dat = echodata::BST1[seq_len(1000),]) } # }

title: Plot XGR peaks — XGR_plot_peaks
text: Plots distribution annotations across genomic region (x-axis).

title: Plot XGR peaks — XGR_plot_peaks
code: XGR_plot_peaks(   gr.lib,   dat,   fill_var = "Assay",   facet_var = "Source",   geom = "density",   locus = NULL,   adjust = 0.2,   show_plot = TRUE,   legend = TRUE,   as_ggplot = TRUE,   trim_xlims = FALSE )

title: Plot XGR peaks — XGR_plot_peaks
text: gr.lib GRanges object annotations. dat Data.frame least following columns: SNP SNP RSID CHR chromosome POS position fill_var Fill variable. facet_var Row facet variable. geom Plot type ("density", "histogram"). locus [Optional] Locus name. adjust granularity peaks. show_plot Print plot. legend logical value indicates whether show legend . Default     TRUE as_ggplot Return plot ggplot2 (TRUE) Tracks (FALSE) object. trim_xlims Trim x-axis limits.

title: Plot XGR peaks — XGR_plot_peaks
text: ggbio track plot.

title: Plot XGR peaks — XGR_plot_peaks
code: #### Import example query #### gr.lib <- echoannot::xgr_example  #### Filter query #### gr.filt <- echoannot::XGR_filter_sources(gr.lib = gr.lib) gr.filt <- echoannot::XGR_filter_assays(gr.lib = gr.filt)  #### Plot query ##### XGR_track <- echoplot::XGR_plot_peaks(     gr.lib = gr.filt,     dat = echodata::BST1,     fill_var = "Assay",     facet_var = "Source") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggbio package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/lawremi/ggbio/issues>. #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `facets` #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf

title: Add vertical lines — add_multitrack_lines
text: Adds vertical lines indicates key SNPs

title: Add vertical lines — add_multitrack_lines
code: add_multitrack_lines(   TRKS,   dat,   snp_groups = c("Lead", "UCS", "Consensus"),   line_alpha = 1,   line_size = 0.3,   remove_duplicated_UCS_Consensus = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

what: Construct SNP labels Construct SNP labels for Manhattan plot. Support function for plot_locus. — construct_snp_labels
title: Construct SNP labels Construct SNP labels for Manhattan plot. Support function for plot_locus. — construct_snp_labels
text: Construct SNP labels Construct SNP labels Manhattan plot. Support function plot_locus.

title: Construct SNP labels Construct SNP labels for Manhattan plot. Support function for plot_locus. — construct_snp_labels
code: construct_snp_labels(   dat,   labels_subset = c("Lead", "CS", "Consensus"),   mean_only_text = c("Consensus", "UCS"),   method_specific_consensus = TRUE,   remove_duplicates = TRUE,   grouping_vars = c("SNP"),   merge_with_input = FALSE,   base_size = 5,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Construct SNP labels Construct SNP labels for Manhattan plot. Support function for plot_locus. — construct_snp_labels
text: dat Data query transcripts . labels_subset Include colored shapes RSID labels help highlight SNPs belonging one following groups:  Lead, Credible Set, Consensus. mean_only_text show text labels RSIDs "mean" Method (method-specific facets). method_specific_consensus label Consensus SNPs also Credible Set SNPs given method. verbose Print messages.

title: Dot summary plot — dot_summary_plot
text: Multi-fine-map summary dot plot lead, UCS /Consensus SNPs.

title: Dot summary plot — dot_summary_plot
code: dot_summary_plot(dat, credset_thresh = 0.95, show_plot = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

title: Dot summary plot — dot_summary_plot
text: dat Data query transcripts . credset_thresh minimum fine-mapped posterior probability  SNP considered part Credible Set. example, credset_thresh=.95 means Credible Set SNPs 95% Credible Set SNPs. show_plot Print plot screen. verbose Print messages.

title: Dot summary plot — dot_summary_plot
code: dat <- echodata::BST1 gp <- echoplot::dot_summary_plot(dat = dat) #> ++ echoplot:: Creating dot summary plot of fine-mapping results. #> Melting PP and CS from 5 fine-mapping methods.

title: Get max histogram height — get_max_histogram_height
text: Get max height histogram plot.

title: Get max histogram height — get_max_histogram_height
code: get_max_histogram_height(gg, round_to = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Get palettes — get_palettes
text: Get unique set palettes pals, ordered preference.

title: Get palettes — get_palettes
code: get_palettes(   preferred = c("brewer.spectral", "brewer.brbg", "brewer.piyg", "brewer.puor"),   n_pals = NULL,   n = 8,   names_only = FALSE )

title: Get palettes — get_palettes
text: preferred Preferred palette names. moved top list. n_pals Max number palettes return. n Max number colors return per palette. applicable names_only=FALSE. names_only return names palettes (TRUE), rather named list colors palette  (default: TRUE).

title: Get palettes — get_palettes
text: Vector palette names.

title: Get palettes — get_palettes
code: palettes <- get_palettes()

title: Get transcripts — get_transcripts
text: Get transcript positions genes range.

title: Get transcripts — get_transcripts
code: get_transcripts(   gr.snp,   max_transcripts = 1,   remove_pseudogenes = TRUE,   tx_biotypes = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Get transcripts — get_transcripts
text: #### Alternative approaches tried #### dat <- echodata::LRRK2 gr.snp <- echodata::dt_to_granges(dat = dat) # Warning:: MUST load full package bc # loads necessary packages namespace. BiocManager::install("Homo.sapiens") library(Homo.sapiens) txdb <- Homo.sapiens::Homo.sapiens GenomicFeatures::genes(txdb) BiocManager::install("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19.knownGene") library(TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19.knownGene) txdb <- TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19.knownGene::TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg19.knownGene GenomicFeatures::genes(txdb)

title: Get transcripts — get_transcripts
text: max_transcripts Maximum number transcripts per gene. tx_biotypes Transcript biotypes include gene model track. default (NULL), transcript biotypes included. See get_tx_biotypes full list available biotypes verbose Print messages.

title: Get transcript biotypes — get_tx_biotypes
text: List available transcript biotypes EnsDb.Hsapiens.v75 database.

title: Get transcript biotypes — get_tx_biotypes
code: get_tx_biotypes(tx_biotypes = NULL, as_filter = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

title: Get transcript biotypes — get_tx_biotypes
text: tx_biotypes Transcript biotypes include gene model track. default (NULL), transcript biotypes included. See get_tx_biotypes full list available biotypes as_filter Return results TxBiotypeFilter (default: TRUE). Otherwise, character vector returned. verbose Print messages.

title: Get transcript biotypes — get_tx_biotypes
text: Character vector valid transcript biotypes.

title: Get transcript biotypes — get_tx_biotypes
code: tx_filter <- get_tx_biotypes()

title: Get window suffix — get_window_suffix
text: Determine plot suffix indicating window size.

title: Get window suffix — get_window_suffix
code: get_window_suffix(dat, zoom, verbose = TRUE)

title: Get window suffix — get_window_suffix
text: dat <- echodata::BST1 window_suffix <- echoplot:::get_window_suffix(dat=dat, zoom=1000) window_suffix <- echoplot:::get_window_suffix(dat=dat, zoom=NULL) window_suffix <- echoplot:::get_window_suffix(dat=dat, zoom="") window_suffix <- echoplot:::get_window_suffix(dat=dat, zoom="2x")

title: List namespace — list_namespace
text: List objects (e.g. functions, data) namespace one R packages.

title: List namespace — list_namespace
text: packages character vector package names. status_filter Filter namespace status (e.g. "export","import"). NULL (default), return namespace objects. verbose Print messages.

text: data.table.

code: nm_dt <- list_namespace(packages=c("data.table","pals")) #> Querying 2 packages. #> Gathering functions from: data.table #> Gathering functions from: pals

title: Generate a locus plot — plot_locus
text: Generate locus-specific plot multiple selectable tracks. Users can also generate multiple zoomed views plot  multiple resolutions.

title: Generate a locus plot — plot_locus
code: plot_locus(   dat,   locus_dir,   LD_matrix = NULL,   LD_reference = NULL,   facet_formula = "Method~.",   dataset_type = "GWAS",   color_r2 = TRUE,   finemap_methods = c("ABF", "FINEMAP", "SUSIE", "POLYFUN_SUSIE"),   track_order = NULL,   track_heights = NULL,   plot_full_window = TRUE,   dot_summary = FALSE,   qtl_suffixes = NULL,   mean.PP = TRUE,   credset_thresh = 0.95,   consensus_thresh = 2,   sig_cutoff = 5e-08,   gene_track = TRUE,   tx_biotypes = NULL,   point_size = 1,   point_alpha = 0.6,   density_adjust = 0.2,   snp_group_lines = c("Lead", "UCS", "Consensus"),   labels_subset = c("Lead", "CS", "Consensus"),   xtext = FALSE,   show_legend_genes = TRUE,   xgr_libnames = NULL,   xgr_n_top = 5,   roadmap = FALSE,   roadmap_query = NULL,   roadmap_n_top = 7,   zoom_exceptions_str = "*full window$|zoom_polygon",   nott_epigenome = FALSE,   nott_regulatory_rects = TRUE,   nott_show_placseq = TRUE,   nott_binwidth = 200,   nott_bigwig_dir = NULL,   save_plot = FALSE,   show_plot = TRUE,   genomic_units = "Mb",   strip.text.y.angle = 0,   max_transcripts = 1,   zoom = c("1x"),   dpi = 300,   height = 12,   width = 10,   plot_format = "png",   save_RDS = FALSE,   return_list = FALSE,   conda_env = "echoR_mini",   nThread = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate a locus plot — plot_locus
text: dat Data query transcripts . locus_dir Storage directory use. LD_matrix LD matrix. LD_reference LD reference use: 1KGphase1 1000 Genomes Project Phase 1 (genome build: hg19). 1KGphase3 1000 Genomes Project Phase 3 (genome build: hg19). UKB Pre-computed LD British European-decent subset UK Biobank. Genome build : hg19 <vcf_path> User-supplied path custom VCF file compute LD matrix .Accepted formats: .vcf / .vcf.gz / .vcf.bgzGenome build : defined user target_genome. <matrix_path> User-supplied path pre-computed LD matrix. Accepted formats: .rds / .rda / .csv / .tsv / .txtGenome build : defined user target_genome. facet_formula Formula facet plots . See facet_grid details. dataset_type Dataset type (e.g. "GWAS" "eQTL"). color_r2 Whether color data points (SNPs) strongly correlate lead SNP (.g. LD measured terms r2). finemap_methods Fine-mapping methods plot tracks , y-axis show Posterior Probabilities (PP) SNP causal. track_order order tracks appear (top bottom). track_heights height track (top bottom). plot_full_window Include track Manhattan plot full GWAS/eQTL locus (just zoomed-portion). dot_summary Include dot-summary plot highlights Lead, Credible Set, Consensus SNPs. qtl_suffixes columns QTL data included dat, can indicate columns one string suffixes (e.g. qtl_suffixes=c(".eQTL1",".eQTL2") use columns "P.QTL1", "Effect.QTL1", "P.QTL2", "Effect.QTL2"). mean.PP Include track showing mean Posterior Probabilities (PP) averaged across fine-mapping methods. credset_thresh minimum fine-mapped posterior probability  SNP considered part Credible Set. example, credset_thresh=.95 means Credible Set SNPs 95% Credible Set SNPs. consensus_thresh minimum number fine-mapping tools SNP Credible Set order included "Consensus_SNP" column. sig_cutoff Filters SNPs plot based (uncorrected) p-value significance cutoff. gene_track Include track showing gene bodies. tx_biotypes Transcript biotypes include gene model track. default (NULL), transcript biotypes included. See get_tx_biotypes full list available biotypes point_size Size data point. point_alpha Opacity data point. density_adjust Passed adjust argument geom_density. snp_group_lines Include vertical lines help highlight SNPs belonging one following groups:  Lead, Credible Set, Consensus. labels_subset Include colored shapes RSID labels help highlight SNPs belonging one following groups:  Lead, Credible Set, Consensus. xtext Include x-axis title text track (just lower-one). show_legend_genes Show legend gene_track. xgr_libnames Passed XGR_plot. XGR annotations check overlap . full list libraries see   (XGR CRAN).  Passed RData.customised argument XGR::xRDataLoader.  Examples: "ENCODE_TFBS_ClusteredV3_CellTypes" "ENCODE_DNaseI_ClusteredV3_CellTypes" "Broad_Histone" xgr_n_top Passed XGR_plot. Number top annotations plotted (passed XGR_filter_sources XGR_filter_assays). roadmap Find plot annotations Roadmap. roadmap_query plot annotations Roadmap whose metadata contains string items  list strings (e.g. "brain" c("brain","liver","monocytes")). roadmap_n_top Passed ROADMAP_plot. Number top annotations plotted (passed ROADMAP_query). zoom_exceptions_str Names tracks exclude zooming. nott_epigenome Include tracks showing brain cell-type-specific epigenomic data Nott et al. (2019) (doi:10.1126/science.aay0793 ). nott_regulatory_rects Include track generated NOTT2019_epigenomic_histograms. nott_show_placseq Include track generated NOTT2019_plac_seq_plot. nott_binwidth including Nott et al. (2019) epigenomic data track plots, adjust bin width histograms. nott_bigwig_dir Instead pulling Nott et al. (2019)  epigenomic data UCSC Genome Browser, use set local bigwig files. save_plot Save plot RDS file. show_plot Print plot screen. genomic_units genomic units return window limits . strip.text.y.angle Angle y-axis facet labels. max_transcripts Maximum number transcripts per gene. zoom Zoom center locus plotting (without editing fine-mapping results file). can provide either: size plot window terms basepairs (e.g. zoom=50000 50kb window). much want zoom (e.g. zoom="1x" full locus, zoom="2x" 2x zoom center locus, etc.). can pass list window sizes (e.g. c(50000,100000,500000)) automatically generate multiple views locus. can even mix different style inputs: e.g.  c("1x","4.5x",25000). dpi dpi use raster graphics height height (defaults height current plotting window) width width (defaults width current plotting window) plot_format Format save plot saving ggsave. save_RDS Save tracks RDS file (Warning: plots take lot disk space). return_list Return named list track separate plot (default: FALSE). TRUE, return merged plot using wrap_plots. conda_env Conda environment use. nThread Number threads parallelise across (applicable). verbose Print messages.

title: Generate a locus plot — plot_locus
code: dat1 <- echodata::BST1 LD_matrix <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix locus_dir <- file.path(tempdir(),echodata::locus_dir) plt <- echoplot::plot_locus(dat = dat1,                             locus_dir = locus_dir,                             LD_matrix = LD_matrix,                             show_plot = TRUE) #> +-------- Locus Plot:  BST1 --------+ #> + support_thresh = 2 #> + Calculating mean Posterior Probability (mean.PP)... #> + 4 fine-mapping methods used. #> + 7 Credible Set SNPs identified. #> + 3 Consensus SNPs identified. #> + Filling NAs in CS cols with 0. #> + Filling NAs in PP cols with 0. #> LD_matrix detected. Coloring SNPs by LD with lead SNP. #> Filling r/r2 NAs with 0 #> ++ echoplot:: GWAS full window track #> ++ echoplot:: GWAS track #> ++ echoplot:: Merged fine-mapping track #> Melting PP and CS from 5 fine-mapping methods. #> + echoplot:: Constructing SNP labels. #> Data is already melted. Skipping. #> Loading required namespace: ggrepel #> Adding SNP group labels to locus plot. #> ++ echoplot:: Adding Gene model track. #> Converting dat to GRanges object. #> max_transcripts= 1 .  #> 16  transcripts from  16  genes returned. #> Fetching data... #> OK #> Parsing exons... #> OK #> Defining introns... #> OK #> Defining UTRs... #> OK #> Defining CDS... #> OK #> aggregating... #> Done #> Constructing graphics... #> + Adding vertical lines to highlight SNP groups. #> +>+>+>+>+ zoom =  1x  +<+<+<+<+ #> + echoplot:: Get window suffix... #> + echoplot:: Removing GWAS full window track @ zoom=1x #> + Removing subplot margins... #> + Reordering tracks... #> + Ensuring last track shows genomic units. #> + Aligning xlimits for each subplot... #> + Checking track heights...

title: Plot multi-GWAS/QTL or multi-ancestry fine-mapping results — plot_locus_multi
code: locus_dir <- file.path(tempdir(),echodata::locus_dir) #### Make dat_ls #### dat <- echodata::filter_snps(echodata::BST1, bp_distance = 10000)  #> FILTER:: Filtering by SNP features. #> + FILTER:: Post-filtered data: 114 x 26 dat_ls <- list(gwas1=dat, gwas2=dat) #### Make LD_ls #### LD_matrix <- echodata::BST1_LD_matrix LD_ls <- list(ancestry1=LD_matrix, ancestry2=LD_matrix) #### Make plot #### plot_list <- plot_locus_multi(dat_ls = dat_ls,                                LD_ls = LD_ls,                               locus_dir = locus_dir) #> LD_matrix detected. Coloring SNPs by LD with lead SNP. #> Filling r/r2 NAs with 0 #> LD_matrix detected. Coloring SNPs by LD with lead SNP. #> Filling r/r2 NAs with 0 #> +-------- Locus Plot:  BST1 --------+ #> + support_thresh = 2 #> + Calculating mean Posterior Probability (mean.PP)... #> + 4 fine-mapping methods used. #> + 8 Credible Set SNPs identified. #> + 4 Consensus SNPs identified. #> + Filling NAs in CS cols with 0. #> + Filling NAs in PP cols with 0. #> ++ echoplot:: GWAS full window track #> ++ echoplot:: GWAS track #> ++ echoplot:: Merged fine-mapping track #> Melting PP and CS from 5 fine-mapping methods. #> + echoplot:: Constructing SNP labels. #> Data is already melted. Skipping. #> Adding SNP group labels to locus plot. #> ++ echoplot:: Adding Gene model track. #> Converting dat to GRanges object. #> max_transcripts= 1 .  #> 1  transcripts from  1  genes returned. #> Fetching data... #> OK #> Parsing exons... #> OK #> Defining introns... #> OK #> Defining UTRs... #> OK #> Defining CDS... #> OK #> aggregating... #> Done #> Constructing graphics... #> + Adding vertical lines to highlight SNP groups. #> +>+>+>+>+ zoom =  1x  +<+<+<+<+ #> + echoplot:: Get window suffix... #> + echoplot:: Removing GWAS full window track @ zoom=1x #> + Removing subplot margins... #> + Reordering tracks... #> + Ensuring last track shows genomic units. #> + Aligning xlimits for each subplot... #> + Checking track heights...

title: SNP track merged — snp_track_merged
text: Manhattan plot GWAS/QTL data various fine-mapping related annotations. Support function plot_locus.

text: dat Data query transcripts . labels_subset Include colored shapes RSID labels help highlight SNPs belonging one following groups:  Lead, Credible Set, Consensus. sig_cutoff Filters SNPs plot based (uncorrected) p-value significance cutoff. point_alpha Opacity data point. xtext Include x-axis title text track (just lower-one). facet_formula Formula facet plots . See facet_grid details. dataset_type Dataset type (e.g. "GWAS" "eQTL"). genomic_units genomic units return window limits . remove_duplicates Remove duplicate labels SNPs part >1 group labels_subset. strip.text.y.angle Angle y-axis facet labels. show_plot Print plot screen. verbose Print messages.

title: Plot transcripts — transcript_model_track
text: ensembld tutorial Gvix tutorial ggbio tutorial

title: Plot transcripts — transcript_model_track
text: dat Data query transcripts . max_transcripts Maximum number transcripts per gene. tx_biotypes Transcript biotypes include gene model track. default (NULL), transcript biotypes included. See get_tx_biotypes full list available biotypes remove_pseudogenes Whether remove known pseudogenes. show_plot Print plot screen. show.legend Show gene plot legend. fill variable name string, color fill plot data . shape Shape type plot genes . Passed plotTracks. transcriptAnnotation Whether show gene symbol  full transcript ID. Passed plotTracks. collapseTranscripts Logical character scalar. Can one gene, longest, shortest meta. Merge transcripts gene one single gene model. case gene (TRUE), keep start location first exon end location last exon transcripts gene. shortest longest, longest shortest transcript model retained. meta, meta-transcript  containing union exons formed  (essentially identical operation reduce(geneModel)). Passed plotTracks. stacking Character. Stacking mode gene region track. One "squish", "hide", "dense", "pack", "full". Passed GeneRegionTrack. method Method use: "gviz" "ggplot". xtext Include x-axis title text. expand_x_mult Expand x-axis limits include partially overlapping transcripts. verbose Print messages.

title: Plot transcripts — transcript_model_track
code: dat <- echodata::BST1  gene_track <- echoplot::transcript_model_track(dat=dat) #> Converting dat to GRanges object. #> max_transcripts= 1 .  #> 16  transcripts from  16  genes returned. #> Fetching data... #> OK #> Parsing exons... #> OK #> Defining introns... #> OK #> Defining UTRs... #> OK #> Defining CDS... #> OK #> aggregating... #> Done #> Constructing graphics...

title: echoplot 1.0.0
text: Migrate ggplot2::aes_string() calls ggplot2::aes(.data[[]]) ggplot2 >= 3.5.0 compatibility. Fix remote URL bschilder/echoplot RajLabMSSM/echoplot DESCRIPTION. Update R requirement >= 4.0.0 >= 4.1. Fix Rd formatting \dontrun{} example guards. Add Rplots.pdf *.tbi .gitignore.

title: echoplot 0.99.9
text: Local R CMD check fixes compatibility updates.

title: echoplot 0.99.8
text: Standardize rworkflows.yml canonical template: enable Docker ghcr.io, set write-permissions. Update R requirement >= 4.0.0 >= 4.1. Fix remote URL bschilder/echoplot RajLabMSSM/echoplot.

title: echoplot 0.99.7
text: Ensure data gets melted first.

title: echoplot 0.99.6
text: Implement rworkflows get_palettes list_namespace pass subset_labels arg.

title: echoplot 0.99.6
text: Make pals Import, remove RColorBrewer. Fix combine ..1$Method <factor<2f980>> ..4$Method <ordered<4e1b3>>. error. Get rid ggrepel: 7 unlabeled data points (many overlaps). Consider increasing max.overlaps warnings. Fixed potential issue getting top SNPs per Method construct_snp_labels. Fixed colours encodes numbers must positive errors. Fix dot_summary_plot construct_snp_labels updates.

title: echoplot 0.99.5
text: plot_locus_multi: New function plot multi-GWAS/multi-ancestry results. get_transcripts_biotypes: New exported function help users determine available biotypes. get_transcripts: new argument tx_biotypes= allows user filter biotypes. Passes plot_locus. plot_locus: changed qtl_prefixes –> qtl_suffixes consistent merge naming strategy. Export snp_track_merged. ROADMAP_plot plotting subfunctions. XGR_plot plotting subfunctions.

title: echoplot 0.99.5
text: transcript_model_track: Handle 0 transcripts returned, without error. get_transcripts_biotypes ==> get_tx_biotypes places. Document functions. Fix GHA: @master –> @v2

title: echoplot 0.99.4
text: Replace “PLOT::” “echoplot::” Add Issues templates.

title: echoplot 0.99.3
text: Fix slice_max bug transcript_model_track.

title: echoplot 0.99.2
text: Removed echotabix dep since functions now handled echoannot. Updated GHA workflow. Nott2019 tracks working .

title: echoplot 0.99.0
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
